content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 30, 1990

Robert J. Boyle
6767 Forest Lawn Drive
Los Angeles, CA 90068

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-120

Dear Mr. Boyle:

On behalf of M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. you have requested
confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

FACTS

M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc., makes contributions on its own, and
M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. is in partnerships with other corporations
which also make contributions. Each partnership is comprised of
M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. and one other entity and neither entity
has a greater than 50% ownership interest. In addition, no one
person or a majority of the same persons in fact exercises
direction and control of the contributions of the partnerships.

QUESTION

Must M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. aggregate its contributions with
contributions made by the partnerships for purposes of determining
whether the corporation and the partnerships must file major donor
campaign statements?

CONCLUSION

Based on the facts you have provided, M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc.
is not required to aggregate its contributions with the
contributions made by the partnerships of which it is a partner.
M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. must file a major donor committee campaign
statement (Form 461) if it makes contributions to candidates and
committees totalling $10,000 or more in a calendar year. If a
partnership it has formed with another entity also makes
contributions totalling $10,000 or more in a calendar year, the
partnership must also file Form 461. Each entity should report
only its own activity.

¹
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Boyle, Robert J.
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Section 82013(c) states if any "person or combination of
persons" directly or indirectly contributes $10,000 or more in a
calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or committees, the
person or combination of persons meets the definition of a
"committee." (These committees are referred to as "major donor
committees.")

The term "person" is defined in Section 82047 to include
corporations. In addition, the Commission has defined the term
"person or combination of persons" as an alliance of persons or
entities formed for the purpose of influencing the voters for or
against the nomination or election of one or more candidates or
the passage or defeat of one or more measures. (In re Lumsdon
(1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 140, copy enclosed.)

Whether M.J. Brock & Sons, Inc. acts as a "combination of
persons" with any of the partnerships is determined by the control
M.J. Brock and Sons, Inc. has over the partnership's decisions to
make campaign contributions. (In re Lumsdon, supra; Regulation
18428² (copy enclosed).) If the same person or a majority of the
same persons in fact directs and controls the decisions to make
contributions from both the corporation and any of the
partnerships, or directs and controls the decisions of two or more
of the partnerships in the making of contributions, those
contributions must be aggregated for purposes of determining
status as a major donor committee and for purposes of the
contribution limitations.

Enclosed is a copy of the 1990 Form 461 and the current
information manual for major donors.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne/P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
Enclosures

___________________________
2 Regulation 18428 was amended by the Commission on June 5,
1989. Due to a non-substantive issue involving other regulations,
it was withdrawn from consideration by the Office of
Administrative Law and is currently not in effect. However, the
enclosed amended version of Regulation 18428 represents the
current policy of the Commission.

(These committees are referred to as "major donor committees.")
